summary: I ran the test-critic step for the "Live pipeline status summary" feature. After installing gems into `vendor/bundle` (none were present in this worktree) and preparing the test database (`RAILS_ENV=test db:test:prepare`, touching only `pipeliner_test`), I ran `bin/rubocop` (115 files, no offenses) and `bin/rails test` (112 runs, 402 assertions, 0 failures/errors/skips — using `PARALLEL_WORKERS=1` since forked parallel workers hung in this sandboxed shell). The iteration-4 implementer fix correctly resolved the prior blocker (`Turbo::Broadcastable::TestHelper` never loading) by switching to `assert_enqueued_with(job: Turbo::Streams::ActionBroadcastJob)`, matching the app's established broadcast-testing pattern, and the suite now passes cleanly including new coverage for `Pipelines::BroadcastStatus` and the live status summary. No worker/TypeScript files were touched by this feature, so no typecheck was needed. I wrote a `pass` verdict with no findings to `.pipeliner/phases/03-build/main/test-critic/verdict.json`, overwriting the stale `needs_work` verdict from iteration 3.
